repo_prior_art.cite: none; doc/API.md ExactID catalogue has no IDA/CPDAG/MPDAG/possible-effect/selective-pivot entry

published_prior_art.cite: GuoPerkovic2021MinimalEnumeration enumerates distinct possible-effect functionals, but does not characterize extremal reporting as a selection event

repo_prior_art.cite: none; closest banked eid_dml_orthogonality_frontier and eid_proximal_phase concern non-IDA report-selection/frontier artifacts

repo_prior_art.cite: none; no in-catalogue CPDAG/MPDAG discovery-certificate or IDA selected-functional proposal

repo_prior_art.cite: none; no in-catalogue two-completion IDA post-extremal Wald-coverage warning

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The flagship object is an affine discovery certificate A_{0,n},b_{0,n}, but §9 computes only an assumed exact graph-screen row, not a certificate derived from a named CPDAG/MPDAG discovery rule; tier=field below novelty_target=flagship.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The conjecture is uniform over local SEMs away from tie and discovery-margin boundaries, but gives no non-corner concrete PC/GES/MPDAG witness with explicit partial-correlation thresholds and certificate rows.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The flagship comparison to GraduZrnicWangJordan2025ValidInference and ChangGuoMalinsky2026PostSelectionCausalDiscovery lacks a location-level anchor tying their exact target distinction to the proposed IDA selected-functional gap.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The statement quantifies over 'a named CPDAG or MPDAG discovery rule' but no rule is actually named in §6-§8, so the objects A_{0,n}, b_{0,n}, rho_n, c_n, and kappa_n are not tied to a determinate selection procedure.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: §10 says to derive A_{0,n},b_{0,n} for a named discovery rule, but because the rule is unnamed the proof route has no concrete classical selection event, test statistic, or boundary calculation to reduce to.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Name one discovery rule, preferably PC-stable with Fisher-z partial-correlation tests and fixed ordering/background-knowledge convention, then define the exact score vector and every threshold that generates A_{0,n},b_{0,n}.

Replace Exhibit 9.1's assumed graph-screen row with a worked three- or four-node CPDAG example computing the discovery-certificate rows from sample partial-correlation inequalities, then recompute the Lee truncation limits.

Add location-level anchors for the Gradu-Zrnic-Wang-Jordan and Chang-Guo-Malinsky comparisons, specifically the sections where their targets are learned-graph/randomized-selection or true-effect post-discovery functionals.
If aiming for flagship, sharpen Conjecture 1 into an explicit rate statement for the selective pivot error, with margins c_n,kappa_n and covariance-estimation error appearing in the bound.

recommended_next_step: Revise Conjecture 1, §9, and §10 around a single named discovery rule with a computed certificate exhibit; otherwise this is a coherent field-tier Lee-plus-IDA instantiation, not a flagship proposal.
